item.title: v0.9.6.4
item.description:
Dictation reliability

Quiet recordings are now peak-normalized before transcription — fixes dictations that came back as a couple of words or a stock phrase on low-level microphones. Impossibly short transcripts silently retry through the Quality model. Audio levels are logged per dictation for diagnostics.

item.title: v0.9.6.3
item.description:
Reliable Bluetooth dictation

  • Dictation continues receiving audio from the selected Mac microphone when a Bluetooth speaker with a different sample rate is connected.
  • The Insights WPM gauge now uses the same percentile scale as its displayed standing.

item.title: v0.9.6.2
item.description:
Focused Notes and typing polish

  • Daily-note generation now routes new information only into sections the user can see, with Notes as the safe fallback.
  • Team aggregate activity sharing is enabled by default on new installations while preserving explicit opt-out.
  • Dictation start and stop cues now use distinct, consistently ordered sounds.
  • Typing passages no longer include accidental quotation marks, and restart guidance is clearer.

item.title: v0.9.6.1
item.description:
Security hardening, browser fixes, and Notes polish

  • Double Shift, translation, transforms, grammar correction, and read-aloud work again in Chromium browsers while Secure Input and recognized sensitive fields remain protected.
  • Sign-in now uses PKCE and callback-state validation; BYOK endpoints and backend provider options have stricter trust and resource boundaries.
  • Notes adds Achievements, renames Action items to ToDo, and improves native hover help and workspace controls.
  • Aggregate Team activity sharing is now an explicit privacy setting.

item.title: v0.9.6
item.description:
Typing practice, feedback inbox, and Notes sorting

  • Practice touch typing in English, Ukrainian, or Russian with timed tests, animated results, and local-only progress statistics.
  • Send ratings, feedback, attachments, and optional diagnostics directly to Sayful's private authenticated inbox.
  • Reorder Notes items reliably from a dedicated drag handle; Action items, Decisions, and Notes are now the default workspace.
  • Typing results and statistics are cleaner and more balanced, with no repeated celebration after returning from Statistics.
  • The Obsidian export action now uses transparent gradient artwork.

item.title: v0.9.5.5
item.description:
Additive Notes + smoother Insights

  • Notes is now an editable daily workspace: manual items survive AI updates, and only new dictations are appended.
  • Voice Context processes one spoken instruction in isolation, routes each fact to one relevant section, and avoids cross-section duplicates.
  • Auto updates can be toggled from Notes and preserve existing content.
  • Insights keeps its animated calendar while drawing the heatmap as one efficient surface for smoother entrance performance.
  • DevTools adds explicit Fast, Quality (Qwen), and Turbo re-transcription controls.

item.title: v0.9.5.4
item.description:
Dictation cleanup + insights polish

  • More Whisper hallucination variants are cleaned from transcripts (stray credit names, subscribe-style outros).
  • Long-dictation polishing aims for livelier, more readable text — punctuation and paragraphing only, your words untouched.
  • Insights: the WPM gauge shows your Top X%; the menubar Language switcher hides when only one non-English layout is installed.

item.title: v0.9.5.3
item.description:
Dictation reliability + rock-solid flips in Electron apps

  • Long dictations no longer vanish into "No speech was recognized": Whisper's stock hallucination phrases are surgically cut out, an empty transcript keeps the recording with a Retry button, and installs stuck on an old local-AI mode migrate to Sayful Cloud automatically.
  • Instant dictation start: the island reacts the moment you press the hotkey.
  • Flips no longer mangle text in Chromium-based editors (Claude Desktop, Codex): replacements there use one accessibility-verified selection plus one paste — atomic and self-checking.
  • No Russian without a Russian keyboard: layout detection now uses each keyboard's primary language, so machines with only Ukrainian + English never get Russian auto-flips or letter repairs.
  • Transcript polishing runs on a much faster model (~1 s instead of 5–15 s); Settings gains a Features command center, an Appearance tab, and a Reset app data button.

item.title: v0.9.5
item.description:
Learning v2 + Features command center

  • Learning v2 applies your personal terms even when STT hears close variants, declensions, typos, or Cyrillic↔Latin spellings, while avoiding URLs and emails.
  • Fix transcripts from History with the pencil button; Sayful diffs your correction and remembers the spelling across apps, including browsers and Electron.
  • Learning suggestions can now be reviewed in Dictionary → Learning, with Learn / Dismiss cards, auto-paused noisy rules, decay for unused rules, and a try-a-phrase preview.
  • Settings → Features gathers feature master switches and required permissions into one command center.
  • Settings → Appearance gathers visual effects, notifications, sound feedback, and scan overlay controls with Preview buttons.
  • Long dictations no longer come back truncated, and silent recordings now filter common Whisper subtitle-credit hallucinations.

item.title: v0.9.3
item.description:
Clipboard restore + Fast by default

  • After a transcript is auto-pasted, Sayful now restores whatever was on your clipboard before dictation — the dictated text no longer lingers on the shared clipboard. This works everywhere now, including browsers and Electron apps, where the previous version silently skipped the restore.
  • The transcript stays on the clipboard for a short grace window, so if the auto-paste couldn't find a focused text field you can still paste it manually; then your previous clipboard is put back.
  • New installs default to Fast transcription (Groq/Whisper large-v3) for quicker, cheaper dictation. If you already picked a mode, it's unchanged.

item.title: v0.9.2
item.description:
Faster dictation fallback

  • Sayful now retries transient speech-to-text server failures twice immediately, then automatically falls back to the other supported transcription model.
  • Groq/Whisper now receives a shorter language hint tuned for Ukrainian, Russian, and English dictation without nudging English speech into translation.
  • Fallback attempts now receive the prompt tuned for the model actually handling that attempt.
  • Speech-to-text failures now include HTTP status and backend error codes in diagnostics, making upstream provider rate limits easier to understand.

item.title: v0.9.1
item.description:
Keyboard-aware dictation

  • Added a Dictation setting, on by default, that hints speech-to-text models with your enabled keyboard languages to reduce wrong-language transcripts.
  • Fresh installs now only enable a secondary flip language when that keyboard layout is actually installed on the Mac.
  • Auto-flip, Always Flip targets, and language menus now respect available keyboard layouts.
  • Fixed a macOS 26 crash caused by reading input sources from a background transcription task.

item.title: v0.9.0
item.description:
Bluetooth dictation polish

  • Kept Bluetooth headphones in high-quality playback mode during dictation when Sayful is set to record from a Mac or other non-headset microphone.
  • Released the recorder's CoreAudio engine after each dictation so headphone playback returns to normal immediately after recording.
  • Added a startup fallback for protected microphone routing, avoiding CoreAudio !dev failures when macOS needs a moment to settle the selected input.
  • Stopped sending empty microphone recordings to transcription and now reports a clear error when capture produces no usable audio.

item.title: v0.8.14
item.description:
Window focus fixes

  • The Sparkle update window is now brought to the front when opening the update flow from Sayful's in-app update button or menu item.
  • Returning from Google sign-in now activates Sayful and raises an existing window, including when the app was minimized before clicking "Open app".

item.title: v0.8.13
item.description:
Team avatars

  • Team leaderboards now show real teammate avatars when the backend returns a public profile image URL.
  • Custom profile pictures are uploaded to Sayful Cloud storage on save, so the avatar chosen in Manage Account can appear for teammates too.
  • Existing local profile pictures sync to the backend automatically on the next signed-in launch.

item.title: v0.8.12
item.description:
OAuth sign-in repair

  • Fixed Google sign-in for the macOS app by routing Supabase OAuth through the saful.app callback page before returning to Sayful.
  • Sign-in failures now reset the in-app button state and show an error instead of leaving onboarding or the profile popover stuck on "Signing in...".

item.title: v0.8.11
item.description:
Quality dictation by default

  • Fresh installs now default dictation transcription to Quality mode, so Sayful uses the cleaner Qwen ASR path unless the user explicitly chooses Fast.
  • The Fast / Quality picker now opens on Quality for users without a saved dictation mode preference.

item.title: v0.8.10
item.description:
Steadier layout flips

  • Double Shift with no selection now flips the whole word even in editors, IDEs and terminals — including wrong-layout words that contain punctuation keys (e.g. F,j → Або, phj,bnb → зробити), which previously flipped only the tail after the comma.
  • Manual flips are now deterministic: Double Shift always switches to your primary language and Triple Shift to your secondary, without second-guessing the target from dictionaries.

item.title: v0.8.9
item.description:
Achievements leaderboard

  • Added a fourth "Achievements" tab to the Team dashboard, ranking the team by total unlocked badges (personal habits and team standing, combined) instead of a period-bound activity metric.

item.title: v0.8.8
item.description:
Team leaderboard goes live

  • The Team leaderboard is now live: /v1/leaderboard is deployed, so the Team dashboard shows real teammate rankings instead of the local-preview banner.
  • Added achievement badges for auto-flip usage (First Flip, Flip Fluent, Flip Master, Autopilot) and for "copy text from screenshot" usage (Screen Reader, Copy Paste Pro, OCR Veteran).
  • Added social/rank-based team achievements: Podium Finish, Top of the Board, Biggest Climber, and Most Improved.

item.title: v0.8.7
item.description:
Manual flip stability

  • Made no-selection double Shift consistently flip the last word before the cursor, including Cyrillic → English conversions such as дела → ltkf.
  • Added a caret fallback for editors where Accessibility cannot read the text value, so Sayful can select the word immediately before a moved cursor and replace it in place.
  • Reset layout-flip typing context on app switches, pointer clicks, and regular shortcuts so stale buffered text no longer affects later flips.
  • Delayed keyboard-layout switching until after focused rewrites land, avoiding intermittent cases where the word changed but the active layout did not.
  • Auto-flip exception notifications now open Dictionary → Learning directly, and Escape cancels an active dictation recording.

item.title: v0.8.6
item.description:
Team dashboard polish

  • Added the corporate Team dashboard with Today / Week / Month boards, podium, paginated rankings, movers, achievement badges, and rank-climb notifications.
  • Added local Team leaderboard caching so the last live board appears immediately while a fresh response loads.
  • Kept Team refresh economical: leaderboard requests run only while the Team tab is open and Sayful is active.
  • Added recurring update checks and a title-bar update indicator when Sparkle finds a newer version.
  • Fixed the DevTools settings freeze by lazy-loading heavy prompt editors.
  • Polished Team animations, centered summary metrics, removed the podium shine effect, and tightened the Team copy.

item.title: v0.8.5
item.description:
Stable word flips

  • Rebuilt no-selection double-shift around Sayful's own word buffer instead of macOS word selection, preventing tail-only rewrites like vj;ет.
  • Kept double-shift reliable for the current word and the word immediately before one typed space, while safely doing nothing after Enter, Tab, or extra spaces.
  • Limited automatic layout flips to ordinary spaces, so Enter and Shift+Enter no longer trigger surprise rewrites while sending or breaking lines.

item.title: v0.8.4
item.description:
Layout boundary hotfix

  • Fixed wrong-layout words with physical punctuation keys, such as vj;tv, so Sayful flips the whole token to можем instead of only rewriting the tail.
  • Improved first-word layout detection after idle by preferring the layout implied by the typed token before falling back to macOS' current input source.

item.title: v0.8.3
item.description:
Faster layout fixes and team momentum

  • Added a Team dashboard polish pass with words-first rankings, previous-period insights, movers, achievement badges, and rank-climb notifications.
  • Added recurring background update checks so Sayful can surface a new version while it is already running.
  • Fixed dictation paste cleanup so successful focused inserts restore the clipboard even when the target app touches the pasteboard during paste.
  • Fixed the first wrong-layout terminal word after idle, including common commands like make, run, git, cd, ls, and pwd.
  • Stabilized the dictation island hover animation and click target.

item.title: v0.8.2
item.description:
Enterprise privacy and quota hardening

  • Hardened token, clipboard, logging, OAuth, and sensitive-field handling for safer enterprise rollout.
  • Kept local history available by default while tightening cleanup for temporary dictation recordings and generated files.
  • Added backend-weighted quota accounting for dictation, screen text, chat, and text-to-speech.
  • Fixed dictation cancellation so Undo can restore a recording while the Undo island is visible, then clean it up afterward.
  • Added clearer notifications when words are added to layout-flip exceptions.

item.title: v0.8.1
item.description:
Hotkey fixes

  • Dictation hotkeys now keep working when LangFlip is disabled. Push-to-talk and hands-free dictation are controlled only by their toggles in Hotkeys.
  • Double Shift now flips only the last word before the cursor, using spaces, newlines, or the cursor as word boundaries.
  • Punctuation keys that map to Cyrillic letters, such as "," in ",hfnbr", are included in the flip.

item.title: v0.8.0
item.description:
Tidy history + shareable logs

  • Manage your history — each day in the Dictation, Screen Text, and Speech tabs has a trash button to clear that day or everything (single entries deletable on hover); recordings are removed too.
  • Share diagnostic logs from Settings → About. The log records what happened, never what you typed or dictated.
  • The Home dictation list opens to your 50 most recent entries with a "Show older" control.
  • Fixed: the Home tab could freeze (spinning loader, dead tabs) with a large history — scrolling and hover are now smooth.
  • Fixed: the dictation island now stays above the Dock on the empty desktop too.

item.title: v0.7.3
item.description:
One-click updates from the title bar

  • Sayful checks for updates in the background after launch — when a newer version is available, a green update button appears in the title bar next to the bell; click it to install and relaunch.
  • The failed-dictation "Retry" label is now white for clear reading on both themes.
  • Removed a stray focus ring on the notifications "Clear" button.

item.title: v0.7.2
item.description:
Always-flip: English and case-sensitive

  • Always-flip rules can now target English — wrong-layout Cyrillic typing meant for English (e.g. "срфеПЗЕ" → "ChatGPT") is rewritten on the spot.
  • Always-flip rules are now case-sensitive and keep the casing you enter, so a rule for "ChatGPT" preserves its capitals.

item.title: v0.7.1
item.description:
Smoother dictation island

  • Fixed stuttering in the dictation island's transitions (expanding to record, switching to transcribing, and collapsing afterward).
  • Recording controls now collapse into the island's centre on finish instead of sliding off to the bottom-right.
  • The animated Home backdrop pauses when its window isn't on screen, lowering idle CPU.
  • Recorder teardown moved off the main thread and transcripts insert immediately, keeping transitions hitch-free.
  • Removed the mic button from the Home banner — dictation runs from the global hotkey and the always-present island.

item.title: v0.7.0
item.description:
Dictation, Home, and playback polish

  • Added a simple Dictation Mode switch: Fast for lower latency, Quality for richer transcription.
  • Added retry for failed transcriptions from both the dictation island and Home history, reusing the recorded audio instead of losing the attempt.
  • Added Home activity tabs for dictations, screen text captures, and generated speech files.
  • Added generated speech playback controls, including pause/resume in the island and Home history.
  • Streamlined onboarding so a ready account opens straight into Home, with hands-free dictation and Shift+Space translation enabled by default.
  • Polished Superpowers hotkey chips, the Dictation hero, Transform demo, and TTS control animations.
  • Fixed LangFlip word boundaries so punctuation keys that are Cyrillic letters on another layout, such as ; for ж, no longer trigger an early auto-flip.

item.title: v0.6.0
item.description:
Personal dictation spellings

  • Added Personal dictation words in Dictionary → Learning, with manual entries and automatically learned spellings.
  • Sayful now watches short edits after a transcript is inserted and can learn safe recognized phrase → preferred spelling pairs.
  • Personal dictionary replacements run before and after dictation auto-formatting, so saved product names, people, jargon, and casing survive cleanup.
  • Cached Insights usage and heatmap snapshots for a smoother Insights tab.
  • Made the dictation island lift animation quicker.

item.title: v0.5.0
item.description:
Sayful Cloud — sign in and dictate, no API key

  • LangFlip is now Sayful, with a new name and icon.
  • Added Sayful Cloud: sign in with Google and dictation, grammar fixes, translation, transforms, and screenshot text all work out of the box — no API key to set up. A profile menu in the title bar shows your plan and weekly word usage.
  • Added auto-format for longer dictations: punctuation, casing, merging sentences split by pauses, and bulleting lists — without changing your words. Toggle it in Voice → Dictation.
  • Added read-aloud (text-to-speech) through the cloud with a choice of voice, speed, and instructions.
  • Added screenshot-to-text: select a region of the screen and copy the recognized text.
  • Added a Manage Account window and an in-app notification center.
  • Polished the dictation island: springier open/close, controls that cascade in, sound waves that ramp up, and a smoother spinner.
  • Refreshed Settings: app-styled tabs, an animated light/dark switch, a per-app microphone picker, and a Dictation-first Voice tab. Engineering options now live behind an Advanced/Developer view.
  • Single Shift now fixes the selected text only; the hands-free dictation default is Left Option.
  • Faster Insights and a dictation history that loads as you scroll.

item.title: v0.4.0
item.description:
A dictation island, and a tidier Settings

  • Added an always-on dictation island at the bottom of the screen (Wispr-style): hover it to reveal the mic and its shortcut, see live sound waves while recording, and cancel or confirm right there.
  • Added a "Transcript cancelled" toast with Undo and a lifetime progress bar.
  • Redesigned the Settings tabs (Behavior, Hotkeys, Apps, About, and Voice) in the new look, and centered everything on wider windows.
  • Dictation no longer shows the flip overlay — the island is its feedback now.

item.title: v0.3.0
item.description:
A real home for LangFlip

  • Added a full main window with a sidebar: Home, Insights, Dictionary, Snippets, Transforms, and Settings — all settings now live here.
  • Added an Insights dashboard: total words, words-per-minute, a per-app breakdown, an activity heatmap, and a heuristic voice profile.
  • Added Snippets — short triggers that expand to longer text automatically right after you dictate.
  • Added Transforms — rewrite selected text with a custom AI prompt via Option+number hotkeys, with Polish and Prompt Engineer built in.
  • Changed extended dictionaries to install automatically in the background on first launch.
  • Changed the default appearance to dark.

item.title: v0.2.9
item.description:
Cloud speech and AI providers

  • Added opt-in Cloud TTS with curated OpenRouter/OpenAI-compatible speech models, voice selection, speed, instructions, and a built-in sample generator.
  • Added Cloud STT for dictation through /audio/transcriptions with provider-side language auto-detection.
  • Added Cloud OCR for screenshot text capture with a dedicated vision-model picker.
  • Added curated OpenRouter model choices for AI text correction and selected text fixes.
  • Changed hands-free dictation to default to Fn+Option and added a picker for alternate hands-free shortcuts.
  • Removed weak or unreliable Cloud STT choices from the curated list, including Whisper 1, GPT-4o Mini Transcribe, and Whisper Large V3.

item.title: v0.2.8
item.description:
Double Shift hotfix

  • Prevented Obsidian-style Cmd+C-without-selection behavior from duplicating and converting the whole current line.
  • Added a keyboard fallback for Codex/Claude-style inputs that do not expose focused text through Accessibility.
  • Limited the fallback to the previous word/token instead of selecting the whole line.
  • Preserved leading spaces when replacing the previous token.
  • Made manual no-selection double/triple Shift deterministic: English tokens convert to the configured target language, and non-English tokens convert to English.
  • Removed dictionary checks from manual no-selection Shift flips.

item.title: v0.2.7
item.description:
Stability after performance tuning

  • Stabilized the first auto-flip after launch so the word rewrite lands before LangFlip switches the active input source.
  • Fixed double-Shift focused-word rewrites that could miss the first character or select too much text after performance changes.
  • Added manual Always flip rules in General > Learning, with a target language per word.
  • Loaded larger dictionaries in the background while keeping deterministic built-in and manual rules available immediately.
  • Reduced per-keystroke overhead from repeated lowercase work and frontmost-app suppression checks.
  • Improved selected-text action latency with exponential pasteboard polling.

item.title: v0.2.6
item.description:
Translation and first-run polish

  • Shift+Space now translates selected text into the language of the active keyboard layout.
  • Fixed Shift+Space replacement so translated text no longer appears next to the original selection.
  • Triple Shift now defaults to Russian as the secondary language, while Double Shift keeps Ukrainian as the primary default.
  • Added optional Screen Recording and Microphone permission rows in General settings.
  • Onboarding now returns to the setup checklist after macOS asks to restart LangFlip for Screen Recording.
  • Disabled speech-to-text hotkeys by default so voice features stay opt-in.
  • Fixed bracket-key auto-flips such as [jxe → хочу.

item.title: v0.2.5
item.description:
First-run setup, local AI, and voice tools

  • Added a first-run setup checklist after macOS permissions.
  • Made Qwen 3.5 2B the default local AI model, with 4B kept as a heavier option for 16 GB+ Macs.
  • Added built-in grammar and copy-text-from-screenshot tests.
  • Improved no-selection Single Shift fallback in text fields that do not expose their content through Accessibility.
  • Added configurable hotkeys, local dictation groundwork, Whisper model management, and experimental OmniVoice text-to-speech controls.
  • Polished onboarding copy, dictionary setup, and release reset commands for testing new-user flows.

item.title: v0.2.4
item.description:
No-selection Shift actions

  • Single Shift can now fix the last sentence before the cursor, even when no text is selected.
  • Double Shift can now flip the last wrong-layout word run before the cursor.
  • No-selection Shift actions are enabled by default and can be disabled in Preferences.
  • Rewrite feedback is more visible: the flip overlay is enabled by default and appears lower on screen.
  • More stable focused-field rewrites preserve surrounding text, blank lines, and cursor position better.
  • Added a built-in short-word rule for Ukrainian-layout бі → Russian бы.
  • Roadmap now tracks user-defined always-flip and never-flip layout rules.

item.title: v0.2.3
item.description:
First-session polish + installable dictionaries

  • Cleaner onboarding with a direct path to permissions, dictionaries, and local AI setup.
  • Simpler menu with direct actions for selected text: flip, fix, translate, and capture text from screen.
  • Installable extended dictionaries for English, Ukrainian, and Russian.
  • Dictionary status now shows active word counts clearly, with update and reset actions.
  • Preferences copy is shorter, calmer, and closer to release quality.
  • Local AI shortcuts enable automatically when the selected Ollama model is installed and ready.
  • Single Shift fixes selected text by default once local AI is ready.
  • Shift+Space translation turns on automatically for a ready local model unless disabled manually.

item.title: v0.2.2
item.description:
Local AI grammar assist + screen-region OCR

  • Single-tap Shift is now the all-purpose AI fix gesture: rewrites the selection if any, else the most recent sentence. Double-tap stays purely mechanical.
  • Screen-region OCR via ⇧⌘S — pick a region, the active vision model drops the recognized text on your clipboard.
  • Auto-fix sentences when you finish a thought with . / ! / ?.
  • Translate selection (⇧Space hotkey + menubar submenu) into a configurable target.
  • OpenAI / OpenRouter / Together / Groq backend (BYOK) alongside Apple Intelligence and Ollama.
  • New onboarding window only appears when permissions are missing — fixes the "looks like the app didn't launch" race.
  • Re-clicking the dock / Applications icon now opens Preferences for an instant visual confirmation.
  • Faster word-flip events: bulk paste + sub-ms gap between backspaces fixes "only the tail of the word flipped" in Slack / Notion / Electron apps.

item.title: v0.2.1
item.description:
Polish + AI scaffold

  • Cross-layout fixes no longer oscillate on real words ("стороны" / "сторони").
  • Standalone "ы" / "э" now correct to "і" / "є" without needing a dictionary lookup.
  • Configurable hotkey: double-tap Shift / right Cmd / right Option.
  • Menu-bar icon replaces the "⌥" placeholder; dims when paused.
  • Refined overlay animation (180° flip, split-spring timing) + Preview button.
  • AI scaffold ready for Apple Intelligence integration (opt-in, dormant by default).
  • README ships with screenshots and animation GIFs.

item.title: v0.2.0
item.description:
First public release

Auto-flip on the fly between EN ↔ UK ↔ RU. Manual hotkey via double-tap Shift (configurable in Preferences). Selection mode for fixing whole sentences. Self-learning Backspace. Cross-layout letter fixes (ы↔і, э↔є). Sticky-shift correction. Per-app blacklist + built-in silence in terminals and password managers. Bouncy icon-flip overlay. Launch at login. Bundled ~45 k UK and ~48 k RU dictionaries.

Signed with a Developer ID and notarized by Apple. Auto-update via Sparkle from this point forward.
